title: rosa × dumetorum var. urbica
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. urbica, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is widely distributed across Europe and Asia. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum var. urbica include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate menstrual cramps, making it a popular remedy for various health conditions. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
